mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within three months triggers recession fears, causing equity risk premiums to spike and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent weakness in housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate revenue growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when housing and credit diverge, or the combined proxy IC drops below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates during late-cycle expansion, signaling robust capacity utilization and strong corporate margins, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises above 95 during late-cycle periods, or the signal loses predictive power after 2008.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate of 2.0 percent validates corporate profit expansion, immediately boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts positive returns in > 40% of instances where GDP growth is below trend, or the forward return spread between high and low GDP growth quarters is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, the cost of capital exceeds sustainable corporate borrowing thresholds, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for > 60 days after FEDFUNDS breaches 4.5%, or the signal shows no directional edge in forward returns across multiple tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth deceleration and unemployment rate elevation signal a cooling labor market that reduces consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict negative returns when payroll growth remains positive despite rising unemployment, or the interaction term IC falls below 0.04 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid recovery in housing starts following a macroeconomic shock restores business confidence and construction sector cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative when HOUST rebounds sharply after a shock, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2010 data.
